title: mentha arvensis var. barbata
intro: Mentha arvensis var. barbata, also known as wild mint or American wild mint, is a perennial herb native to North America, characterized by its square stem and green, toothed leaves. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for digestive issues and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Mentha arvensis var. barbata include alleviating symptoms of indigestion, nausea, and headaches, as well as supporting immune function and reducing stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in topical applications such as salves and creams.
